title: Appositives
description: We use appositives to redefine or clarify a noun phrase; test your knowledge in this exercise!
topic_path: Punctuation: end-of-sentence punctuation, the comma, and the apostrophe

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the correct placement for the commas in this sentence.**

>My sister a fourth grade teacher has to wake up extra early to get to school before her students.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Appositives** are noun phrases that redefine or clarify their antecedents. An appositive might provide a name for an unnamed idea, or it might give additional details.

Appositives must be separated from the rest of the sentence by commas. An appositive at the beginning or end of a sentence only requires one comma, while one in the middle requires two.

For example:

* *Prince, **the famous rock star,** named his home Paisley Park.*
* ***A popular pizza topping,** pepperoni is a spiced sausage made from pork and beef.*

We should separate the appositive "a fourth grade teacher" from the rest of the sentence using commas:

>**My sister, a fourth grade teacher, has to wake up extra early to get to school before her students.**

choices.content: My sister$\redD{,}$ a fourth grade teacher has to wake up extra early$\redD{,}$ to get to school before her students.
My sister$\redD{,}$ a fourth grade teacher$\redD{,}$ has to wake up extra early to get to school before her students.
My sister a fourth grade teacher$\redD{,}$ has to wake up extra early$\redD{,}$ to get to school$\redD{,}$ before her students.

We should separate the appositive "the giant panda" from the rest of the sentence using commas:

choices.content: Tessa is upset because her favorite animal$\redD{,}$ the giant panda$\redD{,}$ is an endangered species.
Tessa is upset$\redD{,}$ because$\redD{,}$ her favorite animal the giant panda is an endangered species.
Tessa$\redD{,}$ is upset because her favorite animal the giant panda$\redD{,}$ is an endangered species
